purpose: Alauda branded visual execution contract for component selection, icon inventory, density, and spacing.

rules: Use <use data-icon="chunk/name"> placeholders; finalize_svg.py embeds the icon SVG.
Do not hand-draw pseudo-icons when an inventory icon communicates the same idea.
One deck uses one icon library only.

connectorPolicy.routeRequirement: Horizontal connector arrows must travel in dedicated whitespace lanes, not inside title rows, table rows, card labels, or dense text bands.
preferredLanes: between group headers and first content row
between adjacent cards when the y band is free of text
through diagram gutters that contain no labels
avoidLanes: same y band as a card title or table row label
same y band as a section heading in the target card
inside the left or right padding zone of a text-bearing card
connectorPolicy.borderRule: Connector arrows must not enter card/container fill areas or sit on top of card borders; keep at least minContainerBorderGapPx from the nearest container edge.

shapeTextPolicy.defaultRule: Text inside a colored block is centered by default. Non-centered colored-block text is an exception and must be modeled as an explicit padded content area, not an accidental baseline/start-anchor placement.
shapeTextPolicy.singleLinePrimitiveRule: Single-line text inside badges, buttons, pills, circle numbers, metric chips, and colored header bars must be geometrically centered, not baseline-offset by eye.
shapeTextPolicy.multiLabelStripRule: When a colored header bar or table strip carries multiple labels, divide it into explicit visual slots or virtual columns and center each label horizontally and vertically within its own slot.
shapeTextPolicy.coloredBlockScope: Includes saturated brand fills and pale tints such as light blue, light green, light yellow, and light red label blocks; excludes white/off-white/neutral gray content cards.
shapeTextPolicy.coordinateRule: For centered primitive labels, set text x to the shape center x and y to the shape center y.
shapeTextPolicy.leftAlignedException: Left-aligned text is allowed only for paragraph/card content areas with explicit padding and an explicit marker such as data-text-align="left" or data-role="callout-content"; it is not the default for label primitives.
shapeTextPolicy.checkerRule: svg_quality_checker.py warns on shape text centering issues when a label-like shape uses baseline positioning or non-centered anchors.

forbiddenTextPatterns: page_intent names such as mapping_table or migration_bridge
density names such as dense_technical or balanced_technical
customerCanvas.metadataDestination: speaker notes, eval report, or file manifest only

platform_panorama: Group capabilities by domain and platform layer; avoid a flat sea of equal cards.
Expose a primary platform thesis or anchor capability so the page has one scanning entry point.
Use compact labels and move long product explanations to notes.
architecture_stack: Show control-plane and workload-plane separation explicitly when the source discusses platform architecture.
Use layered bands with a single dependency direction; do not scatter peer modules without hierarchy.
Each layer must carry a short operational meaning, not only technology names.
mapping_table: Use compact rows with business-decision cues, not only object names.
Include at least one visible judgment signal such as risk, automation level, owner, rollback, or priority.
Do not render internal route or density labels on the slide canvas.
migration_bridge: Make the bridge visually dominant: center panel or bridge band carries the main transformation logic.
Before and after panels may be simpler; they exist to frame the bridge, not to create three equal cards.
Connector arrows must use whitespace lanes and must not touch card titles or row labels.
risk_matrix: Axis labels must be explicit, high-contrast, and readable before quadrant details.
Each quadrant title should be an action recommendation, not a neutral category.
Do not hide axis meaning in low-contrast microcopy.
process_flow: Each step must show a tangible output or acceptance gate, not only an activity verb.
The final step must close with a customer-facing decision or go/no-go result.
Use connectors only between step nodes; keep them outside text lanes.

antiPatterns: Do not replace a matched semantic route with a generic symmetric card grid.
Do not place CJK text in narrow vertical strips or rotate Chinese labels.
Do not let labels sit on top of separators, arrows, icons, or card borders.
Do not baseline-position text inside label primitives; center it with text-anchor="middle" and dominant-baseline="middle".
Do not place connector arrows in the same visual lane as text rows; route them through whitespace between title and content blocks, or between unrelated groups.
Do not let connector arrows intrude into card/container borders; shorten the arrow or widen the gutter.
Do not render eval metadata such as page_intent, density profile, or '样张 Pxx' on customer-facing slide canvases.
Do not make dense pages by shrinking text below the typography ramp; summarize and move overflow to notes.
Do not draw decorative icons manually when the approved chunk inventory has a close match.
Do not create more than three nested visual levels on technical architecture pages.
qaChecklist: Every content page declares route density before SVG generation.
Visible text respects minTextBoxHeightPx and maxVisibleMicroLabels for the selected density.
Related elements are closer than groupGapPx; unrelated groups use at least groupGapPx.
Every connector arrow uses a whitespace lane and respects connectorPolicy.minTextLaneGapPx from same-lane text.
Every connector arrow respects connectorPolicy.minContainerBorderGapPx from card/container borders.
Every badge/button/pill/circle/header-strip label uses geometric center coordinates and dominant-baseline="middle".
Customer-facing slide canvas contains no visible internal route, density, or sample labels.
Icon placeholders use only the approved inventory.
Dense architecture and panorama pages are checked for clipping, overlap, and label collision after render.
